portwatch_chokepoints.note: Daily AIS-derived tanker counts. Anomaly = 7-day mean below threshold vs pre-crisis baseline OR 30d avg (ports avg>=5). T6-S45: 7d mean (was single-day, smooths ~5d feed lag) + strategic ports alert at -40% (0.60), others -50% (0.50).

items.title: China Cuts Saudi Crude Orders as Hormuz Risks and Discounts Reshape Trade
items.summary: Some Chinese refiners have not nominated term crude cargoes from Saudi Arabia for August, while others have not been allocated any term supply for next month, as weak demand in China, competition from other producers, and continued disruptions at...

items.title: Brent Surges Above $86 After Iran Strikes Two UAE Tankers in Hormuz
items.summary: Brent Crude prices soared by 3% early on Tuesday to above $86 per barrel, following an Iranian attack on two tankers of the United Arab Emirates in the southern lane of the Strait of Hormuz, in one of the biggest attacks in the recent re-escalatio...

items.title: Iran Sneaks 12 Million Barrels Past Renewed U.S. Oil Blockade
items.summary: Iran is estimated to have sneaked supertankers with a total of 12 million barrels of crude oil in the week between the end of the U.S. waiver on Iranian oil sales on July 7 and July 14, just as the U.S. announced the renewed blockade on Iranian po...

items.title: China’s Crude Oil Imports Crash to Decade Low as Hormuz Crisis Bites
items.summary: China’s crude oil imports crashed to a decade-low in June as the reduced flows through the Strait of Hormuz hiked oil prices and reduced refiners’ appetite for costly crude. Overall Chinese imports of crude oil plunged by 41.3% in June from a year...

items.title: Oil Prices Have Jumped 12% Since Friday as War Risks Return
items.summary: Oil prices extended Monday’s surge into Asian trade on Tuesday, with Brent Crude prices hitting a one-month high, as the renewed war risk premium crashed the calmer trade of the past weeks amid renewed U.S.-Iran hostilities and the reinstated U.S....

items.title: Oil Refiners Are Cashing In on a Market That Won’t Stay Broken
items.summary: Oil refiners have stumbled into one of the best profit environments in years. Crude prices have fallen back to where they traded before the Iran war erupted. But gasoline, diesel, and jet fuel remain stubbornly expensive. That combination has push...

items.title: America Extends Its Lead Over Russia and Saudi Arabia in Oil Output
items.summary: While the United States has been the world’s biggest consumer of oil for decades, it became the largest oil producer, too, back in 2018, and has retained that crown ever since. The shale revolution and the growth in the Permian oil production have...

items.title: OPEC Cuts Demand Forecast Again as the Oil Market Starts Looking Past Hormuz
items.summary: OPEC has trimmed its 2026 global oil demand growth forecast for the third straight month, even as crude production rebounds across the Gulf and tanker traffic slowly returns to the Strait of Hormuz. In its monthly oil market report released Monday...

items.title: Why the UAE Is Taking a Bigger Piece of America’s LNG Crown Jewel
items.summary: As might be expected in the zero-sum game of global energy, there is much more than meets the eye to the acquisition by Abu Dhabi-headquartered international investment company XRG of an additional equity interest in Trains (self-contained product...

items.title: Oil Prices Surge 8% After Trump Reimposes Iran Blockade
items.summary: Oil prices jumped more than 8% on Monday after U.S. President Donald Trump announced the United States would reinstate its blockade on Iran, forcing traders to once again price in the risk of prolonged disruption to energy flows through the Strait...

items.title: EU Drops Sanctions on Lukoil Founder and Russian Patriarch
items.summary: The European Union will not impose sanctions on the head of the Russian Orthodox Church and the chief of Russia’s largest private oil company, after Bulgaria vetoed the proposal. Patriarch Kirill, a vocal supporter of President Vladimir Putin and...

items.title: Dubai Is Already Planning for the Next Strait of Hormuz Crisis
items.summary: The Strait of Hormuz reopened less than a month ago, but Dubai is already planning for the next closure. DP World is reportedly in talks to build a new multipurpose port and container terminal in Fujairah on the UAE's east coast, creating another...

<meta name="description" content="This issue of the ECDC Communicable Disease Threats Report (CDTR) covers the period 6-12 June, and includes updates on measles, Nipah virus infection, Chikungunya virus disease, Hantavirus, Ebola, and West Nile virus.

thermometer.ethical_notes: Public posts only · No PII storage · ≥3 sources required for alert · 24h cooldown · alerts NEVER replace official sources · TOS-compliant

resilience.disclaimer: Indice experimental heuristico. Ref: INFORM, FEMA NRI, ND-GAIN.

comunicacoes.label: Comunicacoes
comunicacoes.note: Tema severamente sub-instrumentado: 1 de 4+ indicadores presentes.

defesa.label: Defesa
defesa.note: Tema severamente sub-instrumentado: incendios e o gap mais critico (PT/Norte alto risco).

themes.note: Decomposicao em 8 temas da enciclopedia (T6-S26). Score=None quando dados insuficientes. Anti-synthetic: nao fabrica scores. Cada tema lista gaps_to_close_priority com APIs livres a integrar em S27+.
